feed_role: Lab = generator/feed. The clinic SM pipeline (cockpit) owns GATE-1 dedupe, floor-locked rendering, the single preview, and publishing.
note: One creative reused across Instagram, Facebook & Google Business Profile. 10 posts to choose from, diversified by format/hook/topic, each modelled on a niche over-performer and rewritten in EHN clinician voice. Each post carries a routing block mapping it to the pipeline's pillar→format schedule.

pillar_format_map.conversion · Sun: text-led CTA carousel (booking/assessment link in the caption, not on a card)

palette.note: EHN palette only — never the competitor colours/fonts.

typography.note: NOT Libre Caslon — that is the branded-card lock, benched for this experiment.

render_constraints.rule: Shorten copy, never shrink font. The renderer hard-asserts >=36px and crashes the build on an under-floor size.
render_constraints.card_branding: Cards carry the business name "Elemental Health & Nutrition" (green close line) — NOT the suburb. Suburb keyword lives in the GBP caption only (local SEO).
render_constraints.gbp: Every post ships a GBP hook card, including text-only days (Zernio silently schedules a card-less GBP post — always supply one).

posts.visual_recipe: Solid muted sage-green background with white text hierarchy: small all-caps label at top ("CALCIUM-D-GLUCARATE"), large italicized headline (28pt+) with "Why Calcium-D-Glucarate is a Game-Changer for Histamine Intolerance" centered in elegant serif font, followed by supporting body text (16pt, regular weight) explaining the supplement benefit. Bottom footer with small handle (@STEVENJUDGENATURPATH). Carousel indicator "1/7" in top right. Minimal, professional clinical aesthetic with generous whitespace.
posts.angle: Why oestrogen dominance and histamine intolerance cycle together in perimenopause — and what actually helps
posts.build_brief:
Build this as a 7-slide carousel on a 1080 square canvas.

**Overall aesthetic:** Muted, clinical, generous whitespace. Minimal hierarchy. No full-green background — use a soft cream or warm neutral (off-white, very light sage, warm beige) that sits peacefully with EHN green #39B54A as the visual anchor.

**Slide 1 (Hook):**
- Background: soft cream or off-white
- Top: small all-caps label in EHN green #39B54A, 40–44px, Satoshi Medium, left or centre alignment. Text: "THE OESTROGEN–HISTAMINE LOOP"
- Headline: Satoshi Bold, ~95–110px, centred. Text: "Why Perimenopause Flares Your Histamine Intolerance" — the word "Histamine" in EHN green #39B54A italic.
- Supporting line: Satoshi Medium, 40–44px, charcoal, centred, below headline. Text: "And what your clinician should know."
- Footer: bottom-right corner, small Satoshi Medium (36px), charcoal or green: "1/7"
- No body copy on Slide 1. Whitespace dominates.

**Slides 2–6 (Each explanation slide):**
- Background: same soft cream/off-white
- Each slide has ONE clear concept (mimic the winner's density and whitespace ratio).
- Top label: small all-caps Satoshi Medium, 36–40px, EHN green #39B54A or charcoal (alternate for visual rhythm, never purple/pink). Eg. "SLIDE 2: OESTROGEN'S ROLE" / "SLIDE 3: MAST CELLS" / "SLIDE 4: DAO & BREAKDOWN" / "SLIDE 5: SYMPTOMS" / "SLIDE 6: WHAT HELPS"
- Main text: Satoshi Medium, 44–48px, charcoal, left-aligned, short phrase or single sentence per slide. Example for Slide 2: "Rising oestrogen tells mast cells to release histamine." (short, punchy, no filler).
- Optional subtle visual: a single small icon (neutral green or charcoal outline, not stock photo) sitting to the right or below text if space allows; must feel editorial and grounded, never cutesy or clinical-sterile.
- Whitespace: ~40% of the slide should be empty. Match the winner's breathing room.

**Slide 7 (Close/CTA):**
- Background: soft cream/off-white
- Top: small all-caps label, EHN green #39B54A, Satoshi Medium, 36–40px: "NEXT STEPS"
- Headline: Satoshi Bold, ~85–95px, charcoal or with "Support" in EHN green #39B54A italic. Text: "Get Your Hormones Mapped Out"
- Body: Satoshi Medium, 44–48px, charcoal. Text: "A functional medicine assessment can reveal your unique oestrogen and histamine patterns."
- CTA line: Satoshi Medium, 40px, EHN green #39B54A. Text: "Book an appointment with EHN"
- Footer: "Elemental Health & Nutrition" in Satoshi Medium, 32–36px, green #39B54A, bottom centre.
- Whitespace: generous, clean, minimal.

**Colour notes:**
- Primary anchor: EHN green #39B54A (labels, one word in headlines, CTA)
- Secondary palette: warm cream/off-white background, charcoal text, optional soft sage or teal for accents (icons, borders) — nothing bright, nothing that clashes with green
- Never use the competitor's muted sage background exactly; your cream/warm neutral is distinct
- All copy in charcoal for contrast; green appears in labels, highlights, CTAs

**Typography lock:**
- All headlines: Satoshi Bold (700)
- All body/labels: Satoshi Medium (500)
- Never Libre Caslon; never serif fonts
- Minimum readable size: 36px (labels), 40px (body), ~95px+ (headline)

**Overall feeling:** Professional clinical authority, warm and approachable, generous whitespace, editorial grounded aesthetic — mirroring the winner's minimalist elegance but entirely in EHN's green-anchored, cream/charcoal world.
posts.design_recipe:
**REUSABLE EHN CAROUSEL TEMPLATE — Myth-Bust Authority Register**

**Canvas:** 7-slide carousel, 1080px square, white/cream/soft neutral background anchored by EHN green #39B54A as the primary visual accent.

**Layout structure (reusable across any topic):**

**Slide 1 — Hook/Title Slide:**
- Background: soft cream, off-white, or warm neutral (never pure white, never green)
- Top: small all-caps label, Satoshi Medium, 40–44px, EHN green #39B54A or charcoal
  - Placeholder: "[TOPIC LABEL]"
- Headline: Satoshi Bold, ~95–110px, centred
  - Placeholder: "[Main question or myth]" — one meaningful word in EHN green #39B54A italic
- Supporting line: Satoshi Medium, 40–44px, charcoal, centred
  - Placeholder: "[Subheading or promise]"
- Footer: carousel indicator (eg. "1/7"), Satoshi Medium 36px, charcoal/green, bottom right
- Composition: ~50% whitespace, generous top/bottom padding

**Slides 2–6 — Body Slides (5 slides, each dedicated to one concept):**
- Background: same soft cream/off-white
- Top label: small all-caps, Satoshi Medium, 36–40px, EHN green #39B54A or charcoal (alternate for rhythm)
  - Placeholder: "[Concept heading N]"
- Main text block: Satoshi Medium, 44–48px, charcoal, left-aligned, short phrase or single sentence
  - Placeholder: "[Key point or insight]"
- Optional visual: single editorial icon, illustration, or subtle graphic (neutral green outline or charcoal) sitting right or below text; feel is grounded/editorial, never stock-photo or cutesy
  - Placeholder: "[Small editorial icon/graphic]"
- Composition: ~40% whitespace; text sits left or centre, icon (if present) sits right or lower
- Carousel indicator: "N/7" (updates per slide), Satoshi Medium 36px, bottom right

**Slide 7 — Close/CTA Slide:**
- Background: same soft cream/off-white
- Top label: small all-caps, Satoshi Medium, 36–40px, EHN green #39B54A
  - Placeholder: "[Action label, eg. NEXT STEPS]"
- Headline: Satoshi Bold, ~85–95px, charcoal OR one word in EHN green #39B54A italic
  - Placeholder: "[CTA headline]"
- Body: Satoshi Medium, 44–48px, charcoal
  - Placeholder: "[Reason to book / benefit statement]"
- CTA button/line: Satoshi Medium, 40px, EHN green #39B54A
  - Placeholder: "[Action text, eg. Book an appointment with EHN]"
- Footer: business name "Elemental Health & Nutrition", Satoshi Medium, 32–36px, EHN green #39B54A, centre-bottom
- Composition: ~50% whitespace; generous vertical padding around text

**Colour palette (locked):**
- Primary anchor: EHN green #39B54A (labels, one headline word, CTA, business name)
- Background: soft cream, off-white, or warm neutral (no pure white, no green, no competitor's palette)
- Text: charcoal or deep neutral for all body copy; green for emphasis only
- Optional secondary accents: soft sage, warm teal, or muted warm tone for icons/borders — choose one per post, always harmonizing with green, never clashing

**Typography lock:**
- Headlines: Satoshi Bold (700)
- Body/labels: Satoshi Medium (500)
- Legibility floor: 36px (small labels), 40px (body), ~95px+ (headline)
- Rule: SHORTEN COPY, NEVER SHRINK FONT. If a line exceeds the canvas, edit the words, not the size.

**Overall feel:** Minimalist clinical authority, warm and grounded, editorial aesthetic, generous breathing room. Green-anchored, cream/charcoal palette, no clutter. Professional clinician voice.
posts.on_image:
**Slide 1:** THE OESTROGEN–HISTAMINE LOOP | Why Perimenopause Flares Your **Histamine** Intolerance | And what your clinician should know. | 1/7

**Slide 2:** OESTROGEN'S ROLE | Rising oestrogen tells mast cells to release histamine. | 2/7

**Slide 3:** MAST CELLS RESPOND | More histamine → fewer enzymes to break it down. | 3/7

**Slide 4:** DAO GETS OVERWHELMED | DAO (diamine oxidase) is your main histamine-clearing enzyme. | 4/7

**Slide 5:** PERIMENOPAUSE AMPLIFIES IT | Hormonal swings = erratic mast cell behaviour + histamine spikes. | 5/7

**Slide 6:** WHAT ACTUALLY HELPS | Sleep, stress + nutrient-rich food calm mast cells naturally. | 6/7

**Slide 7:** NEXT STEPS | Get Your Hormones Mapped Out | A functional medicine assessment can reveal your unique oestrogen and histamine patterns. | Book an appointment with EHN | Elemental Health & Nutrition | 7/7
posts.caption:
If you've noticed your histamine symptoms ramping up in your 40s, you're probably seeing oestrogen and histamine dance together — and it's not a pleasant waltz.

In perimenopause, rising oestrogen signals mast cells to release more histamine. At the same time, your DAO enzyme (the one that breaks histamine down) gets swamped. The result? Headaches, brain fog, anxiety, flushing, and itching that feel like they came out of nowhere.

The good news: once we map out *your* unique hormone and histamine pattern, we can dial back both naturally — through sleep, stress management, and targeted nutrition — without guessing.

If this rings true, let's chat about what's really going on under the hood.

#PerimenpauseHealth #HistamineIntolerance #FunctionalMedicine #WomensHealth #HormoneBalance #Adelaide
posts.gmb_caption: Perimenopause and histamine intolerance often cycle together. Our functional medicine team in Eastwood, Adelaide can map your unique hormone and histamine patterns to calm symptoms naturally. Book an appointment today.

posts.visual_recipe: Medium close-up of a smiling woman with long brown hair in a casual indoor setting, wearing dark clothing and a red patterned scarf. A bold teal/turquoise text overlay in the lower third with white sans-serif font (medium-heavy weight) reads "The secret to getting kids to open up". Background is slightly out of focus with warm wood and neutral wall tones. The face and genuine smile are the focal point with directional gaze toward camera.
posts.angle: Walking as a tool for mental clarity and connection — functional medicine view on movement as medicine.
posts.build_brief:
Format: Reel/video cover card (single square, 1080×1080).

Canvas & background: Soft cream (#F5F1ED or warm white) — clean, warm, deliberately light to let the subject and green accent breathe. NO busy patterns. Slightly warm neutral tone that harmonizes with EHN green #39B54A.

Composition & layout (REUSE winner's structure exactly):
- Medium close-up shot of a person in a natural indoor or soft outdoor setting (warm, authentic, grounded — NOT a gym or stock photo). Person facing camera, genuine expression, natural lighting. Clothing: warm, neutral tones (cream, charcoal, sage, muted earth tones). Remove any bright red or teal competitor branding from the visual.
- Focal point: face and authentic expression occupy upper 60% of frame; shoulders/upper body in frame.
- Whitespace & density: generous whitespace around text in lower third; text does NOT clutter the face.

Text overlay (lower third, replicating winner's scale & weight):
- Headline in Satoshi BOLD (700), ~95–110px on 1080 canvas, positioned in lower third.
- ONE meaningful word in EHN green #39B54A italic (e.g., "Walk" or "Movement").
- Remaining headline words in charcoal or dark neutral (not the competitor's teal).
- White or cream background BEHIND the text (a subtle card or transparent overlay) to ensure legibility and respect the winner's strong text-anchoring style.
- Subline in Satoshi MEDIUM (500), ~48–56px, in charcoal, sits below headline, short phrase (max 8 words).
- NO text below 36px. If copy is longer, trim it; do not shrink.

Visual anchor: EHN green #39B54A must appear either as:
  1. The one meaningful word in the headline (italic green),
  2. A thin vertical bar or accent line to the left of the text block, OR
  3. A subtle background tint behind the text card (very pale sage or soft green, never full saturation).

Logo & branding: EHN logo small, unobtrusive, bottom-right corner or integrated into the close line.
Close line (bottom): "Elemental Health & Nutrition" in Satoshi Medium, charcoal or green, 32–36px.

Overall feel: warm, clinical-credible, grounded, invitation-based (not salesy). The genuine human face + soft background + green accent = trust + hope. Match the winner's emotional register (warm, opening, curious) while stripping the competitor's teal/red.

Deliver as a single square card ready to post as reel cover.
posts.design_recipe:
Reusable template (topic-agnostic, any functional-medicine movement or lifestyle angle):

Canvas: Single square card (1080×1080), soft neutral background (cream, warm white, or very pale sage) that harmonizes with and never competes with EHN green #39B54A.

Composition:
- Medium close-up of authentic person (face as focal point, shoulders in frame, genuine expression, natural lighting, warm clothing in neutrals or muted earth).
- Upper 60% of frame: face and expression.
- Lower third: text overlay on a subtle background card or transparent tint.

Text hierarchy & placement:
- Headline: Satoshi BOLD (700), ~95–110px, positioned in lower third on a cream or very pale sage background card.
- One meaningful word: EHN green #39B54A, italic.
- Remaining headline: charcoal or dark neutral.
- Subline: Satoshi MEDIUM (500), ~48–56px, charcoal, short phrase (<=8 words), sits directly below headline.
- NO text <=36px.

Visual anchor:
- EHN green #39B54A appears as the italic keyword in headline OR as a thin vertical accent line left of the text block OR as a very pale sage tint behind the text card.

Branding:
- Small logo, bottom-right or integrated into close line.
- Close line: "Elemental Health & Nutrition", Satoshi Medium, 32–36px, charcoal or green.

Palette harmony:
- Primary: EHN green #39B54A (keyword only, not dominant background).
- Secondary: cream, soft neutrals, warm wood tones, muted sage, charcoal — chosen to suit the mood.
- NO pure black background, NO bright or clashing colours, NO competitor's exact palette.

Feel: warm, clinical-credible, invitation-based, grounded. Genuine human + soft background + green accent = trust + hope.
posts.on_image:

"Walk your way to **clarity**" (where "clarity" is green #39B54A, italic)

Movement is medicine.

posts.caption:
Walking does so much more than strengthen your legs. When we move — especially outdoors and with someone we care about — we're giving our nervous system a chance to regulate, our digestion a boost, and our mind some breathing room.

In functional medicine, we think of movement as medicine. It's not just about the physical — it's about what happens to your mood, your sleep, your clarity. A regular walk can shift everything.

Who would you like to walk with this week? Sometimes the invitation is half the medicine.

Ready to talk about movement in your health plan? Let's chat.

#FunctionalMedicine #MovementIsMedicine #WalkingBenefits #NervousSystemHealth #NaturalWellness #AdelaideWellness
posts.gmb_caption: Walking is one of the most underrated tools in functional medicine. Regular movement supports your nervous system, digestion, sleep, and mental clarity all at once. In Eastwood, Adelaide, we help you build a movement practice that fits your life and honours your body. Book an appointment to explore how gentle, consistent movement can transform your health.

posts.visual_recipe: Minimal off-white background with authority figure (professional headshot in circular crop, top-left) beside clean sans-serif body copy. Headshot shows confident middle-aged male with verified badge. Text stack uses dark gray/black 16-24pt sans-serif on white with ~35 words arranged in three short paragraphs. No overlays, illustrations, or visual noise—pure authority + clarity design.
posts.angle: Sleep deprivation isn't a hustle badge—it's a nervous-system saboteur that silences your gut-brain connection.
posts.build_brief:
Canvas: 1080×1080px single square card, cream (#F5F1E8) background.

Layout (replicating winner's authority + clarity structure):
- Top-left: Place a 220×220px circular crop of a calm, confident female clinician headshot (warm mid-tones, natural lighting—grounded, professional, approachable—NOT stock). Position 60px from top-left edge.
- Beside the headshot (starting ~300px from left edge, 80px from top): 
  - HEADLINE: "Sleep deprivation disrupts your *gut-brain* axis" (~95px+ relative size, Satoshi Bold). The word "gut-brain" in green #39B54A italic (not the full headline, just that two-word phrase). Keep headline to ~8 words max, sitting in the upper-right quadrant with plenty of white space around it.
  - Below headline (120px from top), body copy in Satoshi Medium, charcoal (#2C2C2C), ~45px line height:
    "Poor sleep silences vagal tone, disrupts stomach acid, and scrambles hunger signalling. You're not lazy—your nervous system is offline."
  - Below body: soft close line in sage green (#9BAD8C), Satoshi Medium, ~36px: "Elemental Health & Nutrition"

Whitespace: Generous margins (60px minimum on all edges). Text density mirrors the winner—three short clusters, not a wall. Visual hierarchy is headshot + headline + body—nothing cluttered.

Colour palette: Cream background anchors warmth; green #39B54A accent on one meaningful phrase (the "gut-brain" link); sage green (#9BAD8C) for the close line adds a clinical, grounded feel that harmonizes with the primary green (never clashing). The clinician headshot is the secondary visual anchor (warm, human tone).

Borders/shape: Minimal rounded corners (8px) on the overall card. No overlays, no illustrations—match the winner's pure-authority-and-clarity aesthetic.

Render in Canva: square card, 1080×1080, cream background. Add a circular image placeholder top-left (220px diameter). Type the headline in the upper-right with green accent on "gut-brain". Body copy in charcoal below. Brand line in sage at the bottom. Use Satoshi typeface throughout. Add a small EHN logo (unobtrusive, bottom-right corner, ~50px).
posts.design_recipe:
REUSABLE TEMPLATE: Authority + Clarity Single-Card Format

Canvas: 1080×1080px, cream (#F5F1E8) background—warm, neutral, clinician-credible.

Layout structure (topic-agnostic):
- Top-left corner (60px inset): 220×220px circular image placeholder [clinician-headshot]. Position sets authority; face is calm, professional, approachable.
- Headline zone (upper-right quadrant, ~300px from left, 80px from top): 
  - Primary text: [headline-phrase] (~95px+ relative size, Satoshi Bold, charcoal #2C2C2C)
  - Accent: ONE meaningful word or short phrase highlighted in green #39B54A italic (not the whole headline—only the key concept)
  - Cap at ~8 words; preserve white space.
- Body copy zone (120px from top, left-aligned below headline):
  - Satoshi Medium, charcoal (#2C2C2C), ~45px line height
  - Two short sentences (~30–40 words total), plain-English, clinician tone
  - Avoid jargon; anchor to functional medicine evidence
- Branding line (bottom of card, ~60px from base):
  - "Elemental Health & Nutrition" in Satoshi Medium, sage green (#9BAD8C) (~36px relative size)
  - Unobtrusive EHN logo (bottom-right corner, ~50px)

Colour palette (anchored to green, never clashing):
- Primary anchor: green #39B54A (single accent on one phrase—the "link" in the message)
- Secondary: sage green (#9BAD8C) for close line and any secondary accents (harmonizes, does not compete)
- Background: cream (#F5F1E8) or warm white (never pure white, never the competitor's exact palette)
- Text: charcoal (#2C2C2C) for body and headline (legible, warm-neutral)

Styling:
- Minimal rounded corners (8px) on card edges
- No overlays, illustrations, or visual noise—pure authority + clarity
- Whitespace ratio: 60px minimum margins on all sides; text clusters left-aligned with breathing room
- Hierarchy: headshot (human anchor) → headline (the insight) → body (the evidence/translation) → brand line (subtle close)

Tone: Clinical but warm, grounded, editorial—Australian natural palette (sage, cream, charcoal tones). Never stock-wellness clichés or neon.
posts.on_image:
Headline: Sleep deprivation disrupts your *gut-brain* axis
Body: Poor sleep silences vagal tone, disrupts stomach acid, and scrambles hunger signalling. You're not lazy—your nervous system is offline.
Close line: Elemental Health & Nutrition
posts.caption:
Sleep isn't a luxury—it's the repair cycle your nervous system runs every night. When you're chronically sleep-deprived, your vagal tone drops, your stomach acid dries up, and your hunger hormones go haywire. That's not laziness or weakness; that's your gut-brain axis going offline.

The science is clear: consistent, restorative sleep restores vagal tone, resets stomach acid production, and brings hunger signalling back online. If you're struggling with digestion, mood swings, or cravings, the first place to look isn't always the kitchen—it's the bedroom.

What's your sleep like at the moment? Let's talk about rebuilding it together.

#guthealth #nervousystem #sleepscience #functionalhealth #mentalhealthmatters
posts.gmb_caption: Sleep deprivation disrupts your gut-brain axis—and when your nervous system is offline, digestion and mood suffer. At Elemental Health & Nutrition in Eastwood, Adelaide, we help you rebuild restorative sleep as a foundation for better gut health and mental clarity. Book an appointment to explore what's really driving your sleep struggles.

posts.visual_recipe: Solid muted sage-green background with white serif and sans-serif typography. Header "NEW RESEARCH" in small caps (authority signal). Main headline in large, mixed-weight serif font (bold + italic for emphasis on "The Endo-Gut Connection Just Got Stronger"). Subheading in smaller sans-serif below. Account handle centered at bottom. Minimal, clean design with generous whitespace—resembles a research announcement or educational card.
posts.angle: SIBO and hormone imbalance: new functional medicine perspective on the gut-endocrine link
posts.build_brief:
Canvas: square carousel (4 slides, 1080×1080px each). Background: warm cream (#F5F2ED) across all slides—soft, clinical, never pure white. This harmonizes with EHN green #39B54A without competing for attention.

SLIDE 1 (Hook/Authority):
– Header: "NEW RESEARCH" in small caps, Satoshi Medium, charcoal, 48px, top-left placement, ~20px padding.
– Main headline: Satoshi Bold, mixed-weight treatment. Lead with "The Gut-Hormone" in charcoal (95px+), then break to second line: "Connection" in charcoal bold, followed by "Just Got" in charcoal, and ONE word "Stronger" in EHN green #39B54A italic (95px+). Total headline sits in upper 50% of slide, generous left margin, plenty of white space below.
– Subheading: "What SIBO reveals about your endocrine health", Satoshi Medium, charcoal, ~50px, centre-aligned below main headline.
– Small EHN logo (unobtrusive, 40px width) bottom-right corner.
– Minimal visual: a single soft sage-green accent line (2px thick) horizontally positioned mid-slide behind text, extending left margin only—echoes the winner's compositional restraint.

SLIDE 2 (The Stat/Problem):
– Small header: "THE NUMBERS", Satoshi Medium, charcoal, 48px, top-left.
– Large stat: "1 in 3 women" in EHN green #39B54A, Satoshi Bold, 110px, left-aligned, dominating upper-mid portion.
– Supporting line: "with endometriosis harbour excess methane-producing bacteria", Satoshi Medium, charcoal, 44px, left-aligned, wrapping below stat, plenty of white space.
– Minimal icon: a small circular icon (soft sage #9CB388 fill, EHN green #39B54A border, 80px diameter) depicting a simplified gut silhouette, positioned bottom-right quadrant, unobtrusive.

SLIDE 3 (Why It Matters):
– Section header: "WHY IT MATTERS", Satoshi Bold, one word "METHANE" in EHN green #39B54A italic, 95px, top-left.
– Three short copy lines, Satoshi Medium, charcoal, 42px, stacked with breathing room:
  Line 1: "Slows your gut transit"
  Line 2: "Traps hormones & toxins"
  Line 3: "Feeds inflammation"
– Each line sits on its own with ~30px vertical spacing between.
– Secondary accent: a soft vertical rule (1.5px, sage green #9CB388) on the left margin, running the height of the three copy blocks—reinforces left-flush hierarchy without clutter.

SLIDE 4 (What to Do / Close):
– Small header: "WHAT COMES NEXT", Satoshi Medium, charcoal, 48px, top-left.
– Main CTA line: Satoshi Bold, "Get tested. Restore balance." Centre-aligned, 70px, EHN green #39B54A.
– Subline: "A functional medicine approach to SIBO and hormonal health", Satoshi Medium, charcoal, 44px, centre-aligned below CTA.
– Brand close: "Elemental Health & Nutrition" in Satoshi Bold, EHN green #39B54A, 48px, centred at bottom, ~30px padding above.
– Optional minimal visual: a soft horizontal sage-green accent line (2px) above the brand line, spanning the width.

OVERALL PALETTE & FEEL:
– Background: warm cream (#F5F2ED), never pure white—softens the clinical tone, harmonizes with green.
– EHN green #39B54A: primary visual anchor, appears in ONE headline word per major section, the stat figure, and the brand line.
– Secondary green (sage #9CB388): used sparingly for accent lines, icon fills, borders—creates depth without diluting the primary green.
– Charcoal (#2C2C2C): all body copy, section headers, supporting text—high legibility, warm-neutral feel.
– Whitespace: generous, mimics the winner's clean editorial aesthetic. No dense copy blocks.
– Typeface: Satoshi Bold for headlines (700 weight), Satoshi Medium for body/supporting text (500 weight). Never Libre Caslon.
– Shape & composition: minimal lines, plenty of breathing room, left-flush hierarchy on early slides, centre-balanced close. Resembles a research announcement but feels grounded, clinical, warm—distinctly EHN.
posts.design_recipe:
REUSABLE TEMPLATE (topic-agnostic):

Canvas: square carousel (4 slides, 1080×1080px each). Background: warm cream (#F5F2ED) across all slides.

SLIDE 1 — Authority Hook:
– Small-caps header ("NEW RESEARCH" / "[AUTHORITY SIGNAL]"), Satoshi Medium, charcoal, 48px, top-left, ~20px padding.
– Main headline: Satoshi Bold, mixed-weight. Lead phrase + ONE meaningful word in EHN green #39B54A italic (95px+ total). Sit in upper 50%, left-margin flush, generous white space below.
– Subheading: "[SUPPORTING STATEMENT]", Satoshi Medium, charcoal, ~50px, centre-aligned below headline.
– Minimal accent: 2px horizontal sage-green (#9CB388) line, mid-slide, left-margin only.
– Logo: 40px width, bottom-right corner, unobtrusive.

SLIDE 2 — The Stat:
– Header: "[CATEGORY LABEL]", Satoshi Medium, charcoal, 48px, top-left.
– Large stat: "[STAT/NUMBER]" in EHN green #39B54A, Satoshi Bold, 110px, left-aligned, upper-mid.
– Supporting line: "[CONTEXT/EXPLANATION]", Satoshi Medium, charcoal, 44px, left-aligned, wrapping below stat.
– Optional icon: small circle (sage #9CB388 fill, EHN green #39B54A border, 80px diameter), bottom-right quadrant, unobtrusive.

SLIDE 3 — Why It Matters:
– Header: Satoshi Bold, ONE key word in EHN green #39B54A italic (95px+), top-left.
– Three short copy lines: Satoshi Medium, charcoal, 42px, stacked with ~30px spacing.
  [POINT 1] | [POINT 2] | [POINT 3]
– Visual: 1.5px vertical sage-green (#9CB388) rule, left margin, spanning the three copy blocks.

SLIDE 4 — Close / CTA:
– Header: "[ACTION LABEL]", Satoshi Medium, charcoal, 48px, top-left.
– Main CTA: Satoshi Bold, "[CTA TEXT]" in EHN green #39B54A, 70px, centre-aligned.
– Subline: "[EXPLANATION]", Satoshi Medium, charcoal, 44px, centre-aligned.
– Brand line: "Elemental Health & Nutrition", Satoshi Bold, EHN green #39B54A, 48px, centred at bottom, ~30px padding.
– Optional accent: 2px horizontal sage-green line above brand line, full width.

PALETTE (LOCKED):
– Background: warm cream (#F5F2ED).
– Primary anchor: EHN green #39B54A (headline keywords, stat figures, brand line, CTAs).
– Secondary accent: sage green (#9CB388) (accent lines, icon fills, borders).
– Text: charcoal (#2C2C2C) (all body, headers, supporting copy).

TYPEFACE (LOCKED):
– Headlines: Satoshi Bold (700).
– Body/supporting: Satoshi Medium (500).

LAYOUT PRINCIPLES (LOCKED):
– Whitespace-generous, editorial feel.
– Left-flush on Slides 1–3; centre-balanced on Slide 4 (close).
– Minimal visual elements (accent lines, icons only); no imagery.
– Legibility floor: headline ~95px+, body >=40px, any text >=36px. Shorten copy rather than shrink font.
– Brand close on every carousel: "Elemental Health & Nutrition", never suburb name.
posts.on_image:
Slide 1: NEW RESEARCH | The Gut-Hormone Connection Just Got Stronger | What SIBO reveals about your endocrine health | Elemental Health & Nutrition

Slide 2: THE NUMBERS | 1 in 3 women | with endometriosis harbour excess methane-producing bacteria | Elemental Health & Nutrition

Slide 3: WHY IT MATTERS METHANE | Slows your gut transit | Traps hormones & toxins | Feeds inflammation | Elemental Health & Nutrition

Slide 4: WHAT COMES NEXT | Get tested. Restore balance. | A functional medicine approach to SIBO and hormonal health | Elemental Health & Nutrition
posts.caption: New research is reshaping how we think about SIBO and hormonal health. Women with endometriosis often carry methane-producing bacteria in their gut—and that methane isn't just a digestive problem. It slows transit, allows hormones and toxins to recirculate, and fuels inflammation. The connection between your gut microbiome and your endocrine system is real, and testing for SIBO is a crucial step many functional medicine practitioners are now prioritising. If you've struggled with both gut symptoms and hormonal imbalance, it's worth investigating. Ready to explore what's happening in your gut? Book an appointment with us and let's get you some answers. #SIBO #GutHealth #Endometriosis #HormoneHealth #FunctionalMedicine
posts.gmb_caption: New research shows a strong link between SIBO and hormonal imbalance—especially in women with endometriosis. At Elemental Health & Nutrition in Eastwood, Adelaide, we use functional medicine testing to uncover the root cause of your gut and hormone symptoms. Book an appointment to explore your microbiome and start restoring balance.

posts.visual_recipe: A person in dark clothing stands in a dimly lit bathroom illuminated by warm orange candlelight. The primary visual composition uses chiaroscuro lighting (high contrast between dark and golden-orange tones). White serif text overlaid center-frame in 4 lines reads "You were today years old when you found out you should be taking dark showers every night. Here's why.." The text uses medium-weight serif font, approximately 30% of frame height. Background is a real bathroom photo with minimal visible details due to low-light conditions, golden candle glow visible at shower floor level. This creates a moody, intimate, trustworthy atmosphere.
posts.angle: Light exposure timing is the overlooked lever for sleep architecture—start protecting your evening light environment today.
posts.build_brief:
Format: Vertical reel cover / single full-screen card (1080×1920 portrait). 

Composition & layout (mirroring the winner's chiaroscuro structure):
- Background: a real, intimate interior scene (e.g. a bedroom corner, a person at a window at dusk, a bathroom with soft ambient light) shot in dim, warm afternoon/early-evening light. Use muted, cool-to-neutral tones (soft grey, warm cream, pale sage) — NEVER the competitor's orange chiaroscuro or pure black. The image should feel naturally dimmed, not artificially dark.
- Lighting anchor: introduce EHN green #39B54A as a subtle glow, accent, or geometric shape (e.g. a thin vertical line, a soft-edged accent bar, or a small icon) positioned to draw the eye without overwhelming the photo. This green replaces the competitor's orange as the *primary visual anchor*.
- Text overlay: white serif-style text (Satoshi, bold 700) centered on the frame, positioned in the upper–mid section to mimic the winner's vertical hierarchy. Arrange in 3–4 short lines for maximum readability; headline ~95px+ scale on 1080 canvas. One key word (e.g. "blue light" or "evening") rendered in EHN green #39B54A italic or as a bold green accent to echo the green anchor.
- On-screen text (the "hook"):
  Slide 1 (reel hook frame): "You were today years old when you realised your phone was wrecking your sleep. Here's why.." (short, punchy, comment-to-DM hook mirroring the winner's structure). Text size hierarchy: headline dominant (~95px+), body compact (<40px minimum, readable at a glance).
- Secondary visual: a small, unobtrusive logo lockup or a soft green-and-charcoal icon (e.g. a moon, a sleep wave, or a phone symbol with a subtle cross) positioned in a corner or as a subtle frame element. Keep it editorial, never clipart.
- Card close line: "Elemental Health & Nutrition" in charcoal or green, positioned at bottom-right, small and discreet.
- Overall feel: clinical-but-intimate, trustworthy, evidence-forward. The green anchor should feel intentional and calm—like a trusted guide, not a sales flashiness. Match the winner's moody restraint, but in EHN's green-anchored palette.
posts.design_recipe:
Format: Vertical reel cover / full-screen card (1080×1920 portrait).

Reusable layout shell (topic-agnostic):
- Background: real, intimate interior scene in dim, soft-hour light (muted cream, soft grey, warm sage tones). Never pure black, never neon. The image should feel naturally low-light, supporting the message.
- Green anchor (REQUIRED): EHN green #39B54A appears as a secondary visual element — a subtle glow, thin accent line, soft geometric shape, or small icon — positioned to guide the eye without overwhelming. This green is the post's visual identity, replacing any warm-tone competitor accent.
- Text overlay: white Satoshi (serif-style, bold 700) centered, arranged in 3–4 short lines. Headline ~95px+ on 1080 canvas; body compact and readable at a glance. One key word rendered in green #39B54A italic or bold accent.
- Hook structure (copy placeholder): 
  "[Hook statement in 2 lines] [Supporting insight in 1–2 lines]" — e.g. "You were today years old when you realised [topic]. Here's why.."
- Secondary visual: small, unobtrusive logo lockup or soft green-charcoal icon (moon, wave, leaf, or topic-relevant symbol) in a corner. Editorial, never stock.
- Card close: "Elemental Health & Nutrition" in charcoal or green, bottom-right, small and discreet.
- Palette: EHN green #39B54A + complementary neutrals (sage, cream, soft grey-blue, charcoal). No orange, purple, bright pink, or neon. All secondary colours must harmonize with green.
- Tone: clinical, intimate, trustworthy, evidence-forward. Moody restraint, not sales-speak.
posts.on_image:
Slide 1 (hook frame): "You were today years old when you realised your phone was wrecking your sleep. Here's why.."

(Text layout: "You were today years old" — line 1 | "when you realised your phone" — line 2 | "was wrecking your sleep." — line 3 | "Here's why.." — line 4, smallest). One key word (e.g. "phone" or "sleep") in EHN green #39B54A.
posts.caption:
Most of us scroll our phones right up until lights out—and that blue light is telling your nervous system it's midday, not bedtime. Your brain's melatonin dips, your cortisol stays high, and sleep becomes a battle you can't win. 

The fix is simpler than you'd think: dial back screen time 60–90 minutes before bed, or switch to a blue-light filter if you must stay on your device. Your sleep (and your immune system) will thank you.

Ready to reclaim your nights? Let's chat about a wind-down routine that actually works.

#SleepHygiene #NervousSystemHealth #FunctionalMedicine #AustralianWellness #Adelaide
posts.gmb_caption: Blue light from your evening screens is sabotaging your sleep—and your nervous system knows it. Try dimming your device 60–90 minutes before bed, or use a blue-light filter, and watch your sleep quality shift. Let's build a wind-down routine that works for you. Book an appointment in Eastwood today.

posts.visual_recipe: Professional headshot of a grey-haired man in formal attire (navy blazer, white shirt, grey tie) smiling at camera in what appears to be an official government/formal setting with gold-trimmed chairs blurred in background. Large white serif text overlaid on left side reading "I just turned 66." followed by "Here are 5 things I wish I knew at 26..." in varying font weights and sizing. Dark overlay/gradient on text area for legibility.
posts.angle: What I wish I'd known about ageing at 25 — from a functional medicine perspective
posts.build_brief:
Canvas: Instagram carousel (5 slides, square 1080×1080px).

SLIDE 1 — Hero/Hook (Text + Portrait):
Create a warm, professional headshot of a woman or man aged 50–65 in natural light, wearing soft neutral tones (cream, warm grey, natural linen). Compose headshot on RIGHT side of the frame (portrait orientation, shoulders to mid-chest). Overlay a DARK CHARCOAL semi-transparent panel on the LEFT 55% of the canvas for text legibility (no gradient needed, flat 0.7 opacity charcoal #2B2B2B).

On the charcoal panel, place:
— Headline (Satoshi Bold, ~95px, leading +8px): "I'm 52." (left-aligned, start ~80px from top)
— Subline (Satoshi Bold, ~56px, charcoal #2B2B2B): "Here's what I wish" | "I'd known at 25." (two-line wrap, left-aligned, ~20px below headline)
— ONE word "wish" in EHN green #39B54A italic bold (same size, swapped in place).
— Bottom-left corner: "Elemental Health & Nutrition" (Satoshi Medium, 18px, white, 30px from bottom-left).

Background: Soft cream #F5F3F0, no texture. Charcoal panel runs edge-to-edge left side. Headshot fades naturally on right, no hard crop line.

SLIDE 2 — Insight 1:
Left-align a large EHN green #39B54A circle (diameter ~180px) positioned top-left, 40px from edges. Inside the circle, a simple outline icon of a person walking/moving (line weight 3px, white, 80px tall, centred in circle).
Right of the circle (60px gap), Satoshi Bold headline "Move *daily*" (~60px). One word "daily" in green #39B54A italic.
Below headline, Satoshi Medium body text (~44px): "Not once a week. Not for 60 minutes. Small, consistent movement is the longevity anchor." (left-aligned, charcoal #2B2B2B, ~50px width max).
Background: Soft cream #F5F3F0.
Bottom-right corner: "Elemental Health & Nutrition" (Satoshi Medium, 18px, charcoal, 30px from bottom-right).

SLIDE 3 — Insight 2:
Mirror Slide 2 layout but right-align the green circle (top-right, 40px from edges). Icon: simple outline of a plate/fork-and-knife (line weight 3px, white, 80px).
Headline left-align on the left side: "Food is *medicine*" (~60px, Satoshi Bold). One word "medicine" in green #39B54A italic.
Body text left-aligned below: "How you eat in your 20s sets the stage for inflammation (and disease) in your 50s. Choose whole, unprocessed foods now." (Satoshi Medium ~44px, charcoal, ~50px width max).
Background: Soft cream #F5F3F0.
Bottom-left corner: "Elemental Health & Nutrition" (Satoshi Medium, 18px, charcoal, 30px from bottom-left).

SLIDE 4 — Insight 3:
Left-align green circle (top-left, 40px from edges). Icon: simple outline of a sleeping face/moon (line weight 3px, white, 80px).
Headline right-align on right side: "Sleep is *not* optional" (~60px, Satoshi Bold). One word "not" in green #39B54A italic.
Body text right-align on right side: "Poor sleep accelerates ageing at the cellular level. Prioritise 7–9 hours now — your future self will thank you." (Satoshi Medium ~44px, charcoal, ~50px width max, right-aligned).
Background: Soft cream #F5F3F0.
Bottom-right corner: "Elemental Health & Nutrition" (Satoshi Medium, 18px, charcoal, 30px from bottom-right).

SLIDE 5 — CTA/Close:
Centre-align all text on a soft cream #F5F3F0 background.
Headline (Satoshi Bold, ~75px, charcoal): "Your age is *negotiable*." One word "negotiable" in EHN green #39B54A italic.
Subline (Satoshi Medium, ~48px, charcoal, 30px below headline): "Your health is in your control. It's never too late to start."
Large green button/box (Satoshi Bold, 22px, white text, #39B54A background, padding 16px 32px, centre, ~40px below subline): "Book a consultation"
Bottom-centre, small: "Elemental Health & Nutrition" (Satoshi Medium, 18px, charcoal #2B2B2B, 30px from bottom).

OVERALL COLOUR PALETTE:
— Primary anchor: EHN green #39B54A (circles, one italic word per slide, button, close line in green)
— Secondary: Soft cream #F5F3F0 (all backgrounds)
— Tertiary: Charcoal #2B2B2B (all body text, most headlines)
— Accent: White (icons inside circles, text on green button)
No purple, pink, orange, or competitor's navy/gold. The layout mirrors the winner's density and whitespace, but the colour story is entirely EHN green + warm cream + charcoal — clinical, warm, and grounded.
posts.design_recipe:
Canvas: Instagram carousel (5 slides, square 1080×1080px).

REUSABLE TEMPLATE — Topic-Agnostic:

**Slide 1 — Hero/Hook (Text + Headshot):**
Canvas: cream background. Right-aligned professional headshot (neutral tones, natural light, shoulders-to-mid-chest). Left 55% overlaid with dark charcoal semi-transparent panel (#2B2B2B, 0.7 opacity) for text contrast.
Text on panel (left-aligned):
— Headline (Satoshi Bold, ~95px): "[Age statement]" e.g. "I'm 52."
— Subline (Satoshi Bold, ~56px, two-line wrap): "[Opening hook line 1]" | "[Opening hook line 2]" with ONE meaningful word in EHN green #39B54A italic bold.
— Bottom-left: "Elemental Health & Nutrition" (Satoshi Medium, 18px, white).

**Slides 2–4 — Insight Cards (repeating pattern, alternating circle position):**
Canvas: cream background.
Left-side alternates RIGHT, then LEFT, then RIGHT (or follow a staggered pattern).
— EHN green circle (diameter ~180px, top corner, 40px inset)
— Inside circle: outline icon (white line weight 3px, ~80px tall, centred)
— Headline (Satoshi Bold, ~60px, charcoal): "[Topic word] *[Emphasis word]*" with emphasis word in green #39B54A italic
— Body text (Satoshi Medium, ~44px, charcoal, ~50px max width): "[2–3 sentence explanation]" aligned to match headline side
— Bottom corner (opposite to circle): "Elemental Health & Nutrition" (Satoshi Medium, 18px, charcoal)

**Slide 5 — CTA/Close:**
Canvas: cream background, all centre-aligned.
— Headline (Satoshi Bold, ~75px, charcoal): "[Main CTA message] *[Emphasis word]*" with emphasis word in green #39B54A italic
— Subline (Satoshi Medium, ~48px, charcoal, 30px below): "[Supporting statement]"
— CTA button (Satoshi Bold, 22px, white on #39B54A, padding 16px×32px, centred, 40px below subline): "Book a [service type]" or "Learn more"
— Bottom-centre: "Elemental Health & Nutrition" (Satoshi Medium, 18px, charcoal)

**COLOUR LOCK (topic-agnostic, never varies):**
— Primary anchor: EHN green #39B54A (circles, one italic word per slide, CTA button, green close line on cards)
— Background: Soft cream #F5F3F0 (all slides)
— Text: Charcoal #2B2B2B (headlines, body, close line)
— Accents: White (icons, CTA text)

**HIERARCHY:**
Slide 1 dominates with scale + headshot + colour contrast. Slides 2–4 rhythm through icon + headline + body, with green circle as the visual metronome. Slide 5 brings clarity and action. All text respects legibility floor (headline ≥95px, body ≥44px on 1080 canvas). Satoshi Bold for all headlines (one meaningful word in green italic), Satoshi Medium for all body. No serif, no competitor fonts. The feel is editorial, warm, clinical — never stock wellness imagery.
posts.on_image:
**Slide 1:**
"I'm 52.
Here's what I *wish* I'd known at 25."

**Slide 2:**
"Move *daily*
Not once a week. Not for 60 minutes. Small, consistent movement is the longevity anchor."

**Slide 3:**
"Food is *medicine*
How you eat in your 20s sets the stage for inflammation (and disease) in your 50s. Choose whole, unprocessed foods now."

**Slide 4:**
"Sleep is *not* optional
Poor sleep accelerates ageing at the cellular level. Prioritise 7–9 hours now — your future self will thank you."

Your health is in your control. It's never too late to start.
[Button: Book a consultation]"
posts.caption:
I'm 52, and these are the three things I wish I'd started earlier: moving daily (not just weekends), choosing whole foods, and protecting my sleep.

The good news? It's never too late to begin. Functional medicine isn't about turning back the clock — it's about understanding how our daily choices shape our health trajectory. The things you do in your 20s, 30s, or 40s matter. But so do the things you start *today*.

Your age is negotiable. Your health is in your control.

Ready to take the first step? We'd love to chat about what's possible for you.

#FunctionalMedicine #LongevityHealth #AustralianHealthcare #AgingWell #WellnessJourney
posts.gmb_caption: At Elemental Health & Nutrition in Eastwood, Adelaide, we believe your health is in your control — whatever your age. Small daily choices around movement, nutrition, and sleep set the trajectory for your long-term wellbeing. Book a consultation to explore a functional medicine approach tailored to you.

Man holds a peptide bottle in one hand (left) and a syringe in the other (right), with surprised/concerned facial expression. posts.angle: Functional medicine myth: does "natural" supplement X really work the way wellness marketing claims?
posts.build_brief:
Canvas: single square card (1080 × 1080px).
Background: soft cream (#F5F3F0) with a subtle warm undertone, harmonizing with EHN green #39B54A.
Layout & composition (replicating winner's structure): 
- Clinician talking head (shoulders-up, warm clinical setting with soft warm-toned backdrop, relaxed professional posture) positioned left-centre.
- Soft warm/sage accent lighting (never the competitor's teal/blue) frames the head gently; avoid dramatic radial streaks — use subtle warm-neutral tones instead.
- Clinician holds a supplement bottle or product in one hand (left) and a clipboard or chart in the other (right), with a calm, thoughtful (not shocked) expression.
- Bold headline text overlays in two parts: first word in EHN green #39B54A italic (Satoshi Bold ~95px+), second word in charcoal (Satoshi Bold ~95px+) — e.g. "Supplement" (green italic) + "Truth" (charcoal). Position left and right to mirror the winner's asymmetric text placement, with subtle white or cream pointer lines/underscores.
- Subheading (Satoshi Bold, ~60px) in soft charcoal beneath headlines.
- Glossy desk surface: muted sage-grey or soft charcoal (not the competitor's blue), with blurred clinical/herbal elements in background (books, plants, lab glassware in warm tones).
- High contrast maintained, but via green + cream + charcoal, never red + teal + blue.
- 3D depth: soft shadow/lighting on the desk, subtle depth on the head (professional headshot, not theatrical).
- Close line: "Elemental Health & Nutrition" in small green #39B54A text (Satoshi Medium) bottom right.
posts.design_recipe:
Canvas: single square card (1080 × 1080px).
Background: soft cream or warm-neutral (cream #F5F3F0, soft sage-grey, or white) — chosen to harmonize with EHN green #39B54A.
Colour anchor: EHN green #39B54A is the PRIMARY visual anchor — used in headline (one meaningful word in italic green), accent lines/pointers, and the close brand line.
Secondary palette: sage, soft charcoal, warm cream — complementary to green, warm-clinical in feel. Avoid reds, teals, blues, purples, neons.
Composition (reusable structure):
- Clinician/expert talking head (shoulders-up) positioned left-centre, warm professional setting backdrop.
- Soft warm accent lighting (never dramatic radial rays) — subtle depth, clinical-but-approachable.
- Expert holds a relevant object in left hand (product, chart, device) and complementary object in right hand — balanced asymmetry.
- Facial expression: calm, thoughtful, credible (never shocked/alarmed).
- Bold headline text in two parts: [first word] in EHN green #39B54A italic (Satoshi Bold, ~95px+), [second word] in charcoal (Satoshi Bold, ~95px+). Position asymmetrically left/right to create visual tension and mirror the winner's dynamic layout. Include subtle white or cream pointer lines/underscores.
- Subheading [supporting line] in Satoshi Bold (~60px) charcoal, positioned beneath or integrated with headline.
- Desk surface: muted sage-grey, soft charcoal, or neutral (not bright blue). Glossy finish for depth.
- Background elements (blurred): clinical/editorial details (books, botanical elements, warm-toned glassware, plants) — never gym/supplement-shop aesthetic.
- 3D depth: subtle shadows on desk and head, soft professional lighting. High contrast via green + cream + charcoal, never artificial or theatrical.
- Close line: "Elemental Health & Nutrition" (Satoshi Medium, small, green #39B54A) bottom right corner.
posts.on_image:
"Supplement" (word 1, green #39B54A italic, Satoshi Bold ~95px+) | "Truth" (word 2, charcoal, Satoshi Bold ~95px+)
[Subheading]: "What the research actually says" (Satoshi Bold, ~60px, charcoal)
[Close line, bottom right]: "Elemental Health & Nutrition" (Satoshi Medium, small, green #39B54A)
posts.caption:
Myth: one dose of a supplement does what marketing says.
Reality: your body is more nuanced.

Most "miracle" supplements work *if* they're part of a bigger picture — the right dose, timing, liver function, food interactions, and yes, the fundamentals (sleep, stress, movement). One ingredient rarely works alone.

We test, not guess. Functional medicine means looking at what *your* biochemistry actually needs — not what the bottle promises.

Worth a conversation? Let's talk about what will genuinely support your health.

Book an appointment — we're here to clarify what works for *you*.

#FunctionalMedicine #SupplementSense #EvidenceBasedCare #HealthMyth #AdelaideWellness
posts.gmb_caption: Most supplements work best as part of a whole-health approach, not alone. We help you navigate what actually supports your body in Eastwood, Adelaide. Book an appointment to discuss a plan tailored to your needs.

posts.visual_recipe: Close-up talking-head shot of a bald man in a black blazer against a soft blurred grey/blue background. Genuine, engaged expression with animated hand gesture visible. Warm, professional lighting emphasizing credibility. No overlaid text on the image itself—caption carries the hook. Natural, conversational delivery style that reads as trustworthy expert commentary.
posts.angle: Anxiety thrives in adults whose nervous systems never learned how to settle—and that skill starts in childhood.
posts.build_brief: Format: vertical reel/video shot (9:16, Instagram Reels standard). Shoot close-up talking-head of an EHN practitioner (or trusted clinician voice-over) with warm, engaged expression and natural hand gesture—credible, conversational. Soft, muted background: sage-green or warm cream (never grey-blue, never the competitor's palette)—slightly blurred to keep focus on the speaker. Warm, professional lighting that emphasizes trustworthiness and calm. NO overlaid text on the video itself. The hook carries entirely in the caption (off-screen, on-platform). The visual composition mirrors the winner: genuine face, natural gesture, soft lighting, minimal visual clutter—all authority, zero hype. The opening frame (thumbnail) should feel serene and professional: the practitioner's calm expression against the soft, complementary background is the entire visual story. Think clinical podcast—not influencer energy. This reel is about voice credibility and the psychological safety of watching someone speak plainly about hard truths.
posts.design_recipe: Reusable talking-head reel template (topic-agnostic): vertical 9:16 video format (Instagram Reels). Close-up shot of a calm, engaged practitioner—genuine expression, natural hand gesture visible, conversational register. Soft, muted background that harmonizes with EHN green #39B54A (sage, warm cream, or soft charcoal—never pure black, never the competitor's palette). Professional, warm lighting that conveys calm authority and human warmth. NO text overlays on video. The hook/narrative voice carries in the on-platform caption—let the visual do trust-building, let words do persuasion. Composition mirrors a clinical podcast or TED talk: face centred, natural gesture, minimal visual load, maximum credibility. Opening frame (reel thumbnail) is the visual anchor—calm expression + soft background = the promise of evidence-based thinking. Voice-over optional but recommended for accessibility; if used, plain-English, warm clinician tone. Logo placement (small, bottom corner, unobtrusive) on the opening frame only. This template works for any functional-medicine, mental-health, or gut-brain topic where authority + approachability + plain language = engagement.
posts.on_image: Reel opening frame / on-screen hook (2-3 second visual hook before voice begins): Practitioner's calm, slightly knowing expression. No text overlay—just the face and the soft background. The visual *is* the hook: "I'm about to tell you something true that might contradict what you've been told." Caption carries the narrative in the on-platform text.
posts.caption: Happy Monday. Here's an unpopular one: anxiety in adults often traces back to a nervous system that never learned to feel safe as a child. If you grew up without consistent boundaries, soothing role-modelling, or emotional co-regulation, your adult stress response is working overtime. The good news? Your nervous system can learn. Functional medicine and clinical support can help you rewire those patterns. If anxiety's been your constant companion, it might not be a personality flaw—it might be an unmet need from years ago. Worth exploring with a practitioner who gets the whole picture. Let's chat about what nervous-system reset looks like for you. 🌿 #MentalHealthMatters #NervousSystemHealing #AnxietyAwareness #FunctionalMedicine #MentalWellnessAdelaide
posts.gmb_caption: Anxiety in adults often starts in childhood nervous systems that never learned to feel safe. At Elemental Health & Nutrition in Adelaide, we help you rewire those patterns through functional medicine and clinical support. Ready to explore what nervous-system healing looks like for you? Book an appointment with us today.

posts.visual_recipe: Newspaper clipping (The Times) featuring a professional headshot of a male doctor wearing a white coat and stethoscope, centre-right. Bold serif headline "Drug-free doctor: The GP who..." in large dark text top-left. Secondary smaller-font body text in serif font throughout. Blue-tinted photograph of the featured doctor occupies approximately 40% of the frame. White/grey background mimicking print media layout. Byline visible at bottom.
posts.angle: Evidence-based fasting: what functional medicine shows about intermittent eating and metabolic health
posts.build_brief:
Canvas: Instagram carousel (square, multi-slide format). Reuse the competitor's newspaper-clipping layout — centre-right professional headshot, bold headline top-left, secondary body text, ~40% image frame — but render entirely in EHN's green-anchored palette.

Slide 1 (cover/hero):
– Background: warm cream or off-white (never pure white, never competitor's blue tone).
– Headline placement: top-left, Satoshi Bold (~95px+), ONE meaningful word in green #39B54A italic (e.g. "Fasting" or "Evidence"). Remaining headline in dark charcoal or near-black. Headline reads "Fasting: what the *evidence* shows" (8 words max, short lines).
– Secondary line: Satoshi Medium, charcoal, smaller scale, sits below headline. E.g. "Why intermittent eating works (and when it doesn't)."
– Professional headshot: centre-right, ~40% of frame, EHN clinician in warm editorial style (no white coat; natural, approachable). Photograph tinted with a subtle warm or sage overlay to anchor the green palette.
– Visual anchor: EHN green #39B54A border or frame element around the headshot, or a green accent line (left margin or headline underline) to anchor the design.
– Whitespace: respect the competitor's airy, editorial layout — generous margins, breathing room between headline and image, not cluttered.
– Logo: small, unobtrusive, bottom-left corner (EHN lockup in green).
– Close line: bottom-right, "Elemental Health & Nutrition" in green #39B54A, Satoshi Medium, ~36px+.

Slide 2 (body/stat):
– Background: same cream/off-white or a soft sage or muted teal (complementary to green, never clashing).
– Headline: Satoshi Bold, charcoal, ~85px+, ONE word in green #39B54A italic. E.g. "*Intermittent* fasting resets cellular repair" (short phrase, 5–6 words).
– Stat/evidence line: Satoshi Medium, charcoal, ~45px+. E.g. "Research shows 12–16 hour fasts support autophagy — but only if done right."
– Visual: optional supporting illustration (icon or subtle graphic) in green #39B54A, aligned left or right, <20% of frame.
– Whitespace: airy, matching Slide 1's editorial density.
– Close line: bottom, "Elemental Health & Nutrition" in green, ~36px+.

Slide 3 (secondary point):
– Background: same harmonious palette (cream, sage, or soft neutral).
– Headline: Satoshi Bold, charcoal, ~85px+, ONE word in green #39B54A italic. E.g. "*Not* everyone should fast" (short, 4 words).
– Body text: Satoshi Medium, charcoal, ~45px+. E.g. "Pregnancy, active athletes, and certain metabolic conditions benefit from different approaches."
– Visual: optional supporting graphic in green or complementary accent.
– Whitespace: editorial balance.
– Close line: bottom, "Elemental Health & Nutrition" in green, ~36px+.

Slide 4 (CTA/close):
– Background: same palette, or a slightly deeper sage/soft teal to signal "closing frame."
– Headline: Satoshi Bold, charcoal, ~95px+, ONE word in green #39B54A italic. E.g. "Ready to *optimise* your eating window?" (6 words).
– Subline: Satoshi Medium, charcoal, ~45px+. E.g. "Book a nutrition consult with our clinicians."
– Visual: optional icon or accent in green #39B54A.
– CTA button or text: green #39B54A, clear, Satoshi Medium, ~40px+. E.g. "Book now" or link instruction.
– Close line: bottom, "Elemental Health & Nutrition" in green, ~36px+.

Overall feel: authority + warmth, newspaper editorial rendered in green, clinical but not cold. Whitespace matches competitor's airy layout. Green #39B54A anchors every slide. No clashing colours. No pure black backgrounds. Complementary palette (cream, sage, soft teal, charcoal) harmonizes with green.
posts.design_recipe:
Reusable Template: Editorial Authority Carousel (Green-Anchored)

Canvas: Instagram carousel (square, 4 slides).

Colour Palette (LOCKED):
– Primary anchor: EHN green #39B54A (appears in headline accent word, visual borders/frames, close line, icons).
– Secondary palette: cream or off-white base + soft sage, muted teal, or warm neutral accents. No clashing colours (no purple, bright pink, orange, strong red, neon). Backgrounds: cream, off-white, soft sage, muted teal, charcoal-neutral — never pure black, never competitor's exact blues.

Layout & Hierarchy (matched to competitor's newspaper structure):
– Professional image placement: centre-right of frame, ~40% of slide width. Editorial headshot in warm, grounded style (no stock clichés). Subtle warm or sage overlay to anchor the green palette.
– Headline placement: top-left, Satoshi Bold (~95px+), ONE meaningful word in green #39B54A italic, remaining text in dark charcoal. Short lines (5–8 words per line).
– Secondary line: Satoshi Medium, charcoal, smaller scale, sits below headline (supporting phrase, 6–10 words).
– Body/stat text: Satoshi Medium, charcoal, ~45px+ for legibility, short phrases (one sentence per slide).
– Visual anchors: green #39B54A border frame around image, left margin accent line, or icon in green — positioned to guide the eye without clutter.
– Whitespace: generous margins, breathing room between headline and image, editorial density (not cluttered, not sparse).
– Close line: bottom-right (Slide 1) or bottom-centre (Slides 2–4), "Elemental Health & Nutrition" in green #39B54A, Satoshi Medium, ~36px+.

Slide Sequence:
– Slide 1 (hero/cover): [headline] + [supporting line] + [professional headshot, centre-right] + [green visual anchor]. Warm cream/off-white background.
– Slide 2 (stat/evidence): [headline with one word in green] + [stat or evidence body text] + [optional icon/illustration in green, <20% frame]. Sage or soft teal background.
– Slide 3 (secondary point): [headline with one word in green] + [supporting body text] + [optional supporting graphic]. Same or complementary background.
– Slide 4 (CTA/close): [closing headline with one word in green] + [CTA subline] + [optional green accent element]. Slightly deeper or signal background (sage/teal deeper tone, or cream).

Logo & Branding:
– EHN lockup: small, unobtrusive, bottom-left (Slide 1) or integrated into close line.
– Close line "Elemental Health & Nutrition" in green #39B54A on every slide.

Typography LOCKED:
– Headlines: Satoshi Bold (700), ~95px+ for main headline, ~85px+ for secondary. ONE meaningful word in green #39B54A italic.
– Body/secondary: Satoshi Medium (500), charcoal, ~45px+ for stats, ~40px+ for body text. Never sub-36px.
– Rule: SHORTEN COPY, NEVER SHRINK FONT. Express size as relative hierarchy, not point size.

Feel: Authority + warmth. Editorial, clinical-but-approachable. Green anchors the entire sequence. Whitespace mirrors competitor's newspaper layout. No pure black, no clashing palette. Airy, grounded, professional.
posts.on_image:
Slide 1: Fasting: what the *evidence* shows | Why intermittent eating works (and when it doesn't) | [professional headshot centre-right, green border frame] | Elemental Health & Nutrition

Slide 2: *Intermittent* fasting resets cellular repair | Research shows 12–16 hour fasts support autophagy — but only if done right | [optional green icon] | Elemental Health & Nutrition

Slide 3: *Not* everyone should fast | Pregnancy, active athletes, and certain metabolic conditions benefit from different eating patterns | [optional supporting graphic] | Elemental Health & Nutrition

Slide 4: Ready to *optimise* your eating window? | Book a nutrition consult with our clinicians | [green CTA or accent element] | Elemental Health & Nutrition
posts.caption:
Fasting isn't one-size-fits-all — and that's where functional medicine shines. The evidence is clear: intermittent eating can support metabolic health and cellular repair, but *when* you eat, *how long* you fast, and *your* individual health status all matter.

We work with each client to design an eating pattern that fits their body, their goals, and their life — not a trend. Whether you're curious about fasting or ready to reset your relationship with food, our clinicians can help.

Ready to explore? Book a nutrition consult with us in Adelaide.

#IntermittentFasting #FunctionalNutrition #MetabolicHealth #EvidenceBasedWellness #AdelaideNutrition
posts.gmb_caption: Fasting isn't one-size-fits-all — and functional medicine proves it. Our clinicians in Eastwood, Adelaide design personalised eating patterns based on evidence, not trends. Book a nutrition consult today to discover what works for your body.

posts.visual_recipe: Text-only Facebook post (no image available). Multi-paragraph caption with a myth-busting structure: opens with a clinical insight ("commonly missed root cause"), then reframes symptom presentation to validate reader experience (fatigue, brain fog, anxiety, weight issues aren't just stress/aging), and closes with a value-driven CTA (comment to unlock). No visual elements, pure copy-driven engagement model relying on authority and permission-gated content offer.
posts.angle: Mould toxicity is a commonly missed root cause of fatigue, brain fog, and weight gain — here's what to look for.
posts.build_brief:
Canvas: Single text-only Facebook/Instagram card (square 1080×1080 px). 

Background: Warm cream (#FAF8F4) or soft white to complement green anchor.

Layout: Multi-paragraph text block centred in the card, with intentional whitespace above, between paragraphs, and below. Mimic the winner's density: generous line spacing (1.6–1.8), paragraph breaks creating visual rhythm, no image.

Hierarchy:
— Opening hook (Satoshi Bold, ~95px): "One word in green #39B54A italic" (e.g. *"Missed"*), rest charcoal #2C2C2C. Line: "A commonly *missed* root cause". ~1–2 lines.
— Validation paragraph (Satoshi Medium, ~48px, charcoal): Reframe symptoms (fatigue, brain fog, anxiety, weight issues) as potentially mould-linked, not just stress/aging. 2–3 lines, warm and clinical. Start a new paragraph.
— Clinical insight paragraph (Satoshi Medium, ~48px, charcoal): Brief, plain-English explanation of how mould toxins can trigger systemic symptoms. 1–2 lines, permission-gated tone.
— CTA paragraph (Satoshi Medium, ~48px, green #39B54A): "Comment below to unlock our mould assessment checklist." 1 line.

Bottom: Satoshi Medium, ~40px, charcoal: "Elemental Health & Nutrition" (never suburb). Small, discreet brand lockup (logo, ~40×40 px) to the left or embedded.

Colour palette: 
— Primary anchor: EHN Green #39B54A (headline callout, CTA text, small logo accent).
— Secondary: Sage #A8B8A0 (optional subtle accent line or divider, if needed for visual rhythm).
— Neutrals: Charcoal #2C2C2C (body), cream #FAF8F4 (background).
— NO competitor's purples, reds, or pinks. NO pure black.

Whitespace: Match the winner's open, breathing layout. Ensure each paragraph has clear vertical separation. The card should feel clinical and warm, not dense.

Feel: Editorial, permission-gated, authority-driven. No emojis. No graphics. Type-only, like the winner.
posts.design_recipe:
**EHN Text-Only Permission-Gated Card (Reusable Template)**

Canvas: Square 1080×1080 px (Instagram/Facebook native).

Background: Warm cream or soft white, chosen to harmonize with EHN Green #39B54A.

Layout: Centred text block with generous whitespace above, between paragraphs, and below. Multi-paragraph structure with clear visual rhythm (line spacing 1.6–1.8, paragraph breaks creating breathing room). No image, no graphics—type-driven.

Hierarchy:
— Headline (Satoshi Bold, ~95px relative scale): [ONE MEANINGFUL WORD] in green #39B54A italic; rest in charcoal #2C2C2C. Short, punchy—1–2 lines max.
— Validation paragraph (Satoshi Medium, ~48px relative scale): Reframe [SYMPTOM CLUSTER] as potentially [FUNCTIONAL ROOT CAUSE], validating reader experience. 2–3 lines, warm + clinical.
— Clinical insight paragraph (Satoshi Medium, ~48px relative scale): Plain-English, permission-gated explanation of [MECHANISM]. 1–2 lines.
— CTA paragraph (Satoshi Medium, ~48px relative scale, green #39B54A): "Comment below to unlock [OFFER NAME]." 1 line.

Footer: Brand line "Elemental Health & Nutrition" (Satoshi Medium, ~40px, charcoal) + small logo lockup (~40×40 px) to the left. Discreet, never competing with headline.

Colour palette:
— Primary anchor: EHN Green #39B54A (headline callout word, CTA text, logo accent).
— Secondary (optional): Sage #A8B8A0 or soft teal (subtle divider or whitespace accent).
— Neutrals: Charcoal #2C2C2C (body copy), cream/white (background).
— Hard rule: Never pure black, never competitor's palette, never clashing colours (purple, bright pink, orange, strong red).

Type: Satoshi throughout. Headlines BOLD (700), body MEDIUM (500). No Libre Caslon on this format. Legibility floor: headline ≥95px relative, body ≥48px, footer ≥40px. If copy is long, shorten—never shrink font.

Feel: Editorial, authority-driven, permission-gated. Clinical-warm. No emojis, no graphics, no clichés. Pure copy engagement.
posts.on_image:
**Headline (on card):**
A commonly *missed* root cause

**Body copy (on card, paragraph breaks):**
Fatigue that won't budge. Brain fog. Anxiety. Weight that sticks despite your efforts. These often get labelled stress or aging. But mould toxicity—one of the most overlooked drivers in functional medicine—can look exactly like this.

**Supporting paragraph:**
When we're exposed to mould spores or mycotoxins (the poisons mould produces), our immune system can mount a persistent inflammatory response. This exhausts your energy, clouds your thinking, and destabilises your metabolism.

**CTA:**
Comment below to unlock our mould assessment checklist.

**Footer:**
Elemental Health & Nutrition
posts.caption:
Mould toxicity is one of the most commonly overlooked root causes I see in functional medicine—and it rarely looks like coughing.

Instead, it often shows up as relentless fatigue, brain fog, anxiety, or weight that won't shift despite your best efforts. Many of my patients have spent years being told these are just stress or aging, when the real driver is chronic mould exposure or mycotoxin sensitivity.

The immune response to mould can trigger systemic inflammation, exhaust your energy, cloud your thinking, and dysregulate your metabolism. If you've had water damage, live in a damp building, or have a history of mould exposure, this might be worth exploring.

Comment below and we'll send you our mould assessment checklist—it helps you recognise the patterns and decide whether this is worth investigating further.

#MouldToxicity #FunctionalMedicine #RootCauseAnalysis #BrainFog #ChronisFatigue #AdelaideHealthClinic
posts.gmb_caption: Mould toxicity is often missed—but it can show up as persistent fatigue, brain fog, and stubborn weight gain. If you've had exposure or live in a damp environment, it's worth investigating. Book an appointment with our team in Eastwood, Adelaide to explore your root causes.

routing.gate1_note: Topic "toxins/mould/microplastics" ran in EHN's last ~4 weeks — pipeline GATE-1 will likely collapse/drop this pick.
